test.command: python3 - <<'PY' ... worker-dispatch structural assertions over mission-worker.md, code-quality-worker.md, mission-orchestrator/SKILL.md, and mission-run.toml ... PY

typecheck.command: python3 - <<'PY' ... validate agent frontmatter/tool names and parse commands/mission-run.toml with tomllib ... PY

lint.command: bash -n scripts/scaffold-mission.sh && bash -n scripts/validate-state.sh && cd /Users/codex/Projects/gemini-mission-control-extension && gemini extensions link .

blockingIssues.description: agents/mission-worker.md limits discoveredIssues severities to blocking/non_blocking, which is too coarse for the worker-dispatch decision tree described elsewhere in the mission artifacts.
agents/code-quality-worker.md repeats the same blocking/non_blocking-only severity model, leaving no way to distinguish follow-up-worthy findings from misc-milestone issues.

skills/mission-orchestrator/SKILL.md injects scrutiny validator examples whose preconditions are prose strings instead of feature IDs, so the validators would not become ready under the skill's own DAG evaluation rules.
Step 8 treats milestones with only cancelled implementation features as ready for validator injection, which contradicts the later rule that fully cancelled milestones need no validation.
commands/mission-run.toml allows /mission-run to proceed from worker_running even though the feature contract required an orchestrator_turn-only guard with an error for any other state.
commands/mission-run.toml also allows handoff_review even though the orchestrator skill prerequisites and crash-recovery flow do not support starting from that state.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify how mission feature skillName values should map to actually installed worker skills, and tell workers where project-local skills live before they conclude a named skill is unavailable.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: All three worker-dispatch reviews reported the same mismatch: mission features reference skillName 'extension-builder', worker transcripts show Skill activation failing with 'Skill "extension-builder" not found', yet the repo contains .factory/skills/extension-builder/SKILL.md.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Choose and document one canonical issue-severity taxonomy across worker handoffs, orchestrator routing, and GEMINI.md so downstream decision logic is unambiguous.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The worker-dispatch reviews found GEMINI.md describing high/critical vs low/medium routing while worker handoffs and the orchestrator skill use blocking/non_blocking, leading to incompatible expectations for discoveredIssues severity.
